summary: iOS | Intermittent | DFU/Language Change fails at >90%
scenario: Device intermittently reverts to previous firmware or language after a DFU or language change completes at >90%, showing as updated until restart.
description_clean: Intermittent failure of DFU or Language Change at >90% completion. After DFU, device shows as up to date but reverts to outdated firmware after restart. After Language Change, device may briefly show new language then restart and revert to previous language. Issue is random and intermittent.
steps: Perform a DFU (Device Firmware Update) or Language Change operation on the device.
Observe the progress of the operation until it reaches >90% completion.
Note if the operation fails intermittently at this stage.
If DFU: After completion, check that the device shows as 'up to date'.
Check the firmware version after restart to see if it has reverted to the outdated firmware.
If Language Change: After completion, observe if the device briefly displays the new language.
Restart the device or wait for an automatic restart.
Check the language setting after restart to see if it has reverted to the previous language.
expected: The DFU or Language Change operation completes successfully at >90% completion. After DFU, the device remains on the updated firmware after restart. After Language Change, the device retains the new language after restart.
actual: Intermittent failure of DFU or Language Change at >90% completion. After DFU, device shows as up to date but reverts to outdated firmware after restart. After Language Change, device may briefly show new language then restart and revert to previous language. Issue is random and intermittent.
